subject: Accounting
title: The Chart of Accounts
description: One-minute skill: The Chart of Accounts.

hook.text: Every company has a master list of all its accounts—like a filing system for money.

buildup.text: The chart of accounts organizes accounts into categories: assets, liabilities, equity, revenue, expenses.

discovery.text: Each account gets a unique number—making it easy to sort, reference, and report on transactions.

twist.text: A messy chart of accounts creates confusion. A well-designed one makes reporting effortless.

climax.text: The chart is typically set up once and updated as the business grows and adds new types of transactions.

punchline.text: Organize the accounts first. Everything else follows.

quiz.question: What is a chart of accounts?
options: A master list organizing all of a company's accounts
A list of employees and their salaries
